title: Plinko
tagline: Drop the ball; where it lands is pure binomial luck.
mechanic: The ball takes `rows` left/right bounces, landing in bucket b with probability C(rows,b)/2^rows. The payout table is solved (not guessed) so Σ P(b)·payout(b) = RTP. No decisions — it's a variance dial via the risk level.
showcases: SimpleMath with no player decision (pure variance)
a payout table solved to a target RTP
mathTest: No decisions to make, so RTP is whatever the table integrates to — the simulator confirms it lands on 0.99 for every rows/risk combination.
